steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.mundare: 'mundare' is an infinitive (to cleanse), not a finite verb
hints.nolo: 'nolo' means 'I do not want' but does not appear in this phrase
hints.velle: 'velle' is the infinitive of 'volo' — 'Volo' is the finite form

hints.to fly: 'to fly' is 'volare' — 'Volo' here comes from 'velle' (to wish)

hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'volui' — 'Volo' is present indicative 1st person
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'velim' — 'Volo' is present indicative

hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to fly) — different verb!
hints.volvo, volvere, volvi, volutum: These are principal parts of 'volvo' (to roll)
hints.valeo, valere, valui, valitum: These are principal parts of 'valeo' (to be strong)

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'munda' — 'mundare' ending in -are is the infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'mundans' — 'mundare' is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'mundandi' — 'mundare' is the present active infinitive

hints.to build a wall: 'to build a wall' is 'munire/murare' — 'mundare' means 'to cleanse'

hints.munio, munire, munivi, munitum: These are principal parts of 'munio' (to fortify)
hints.moneo, monere, monui, monitum: These are principal parts of 'moneo' (to warn)
hints.muto, mutare, mutavi, mutatum: These are principal parts of 'muto' (to change)
